examples: Doctors training for General Practice must complete programmes in a number of specialties, including Paediatrics.
It was only with the growth of paediatrics and psychology in the twentieth century that studies could be carried out on a more objective, if still not always very scientific, basis.
The Royal College of Paediatrics and Child Health strongly supports the use of folic acid in pregnancy.
In-patient paediatrics, obstetrics, accident and emergency services are all under threat at the Kettering hospital.
Adam Finn, a professor of paediatrics at the University of Bristol, agrees that a new strategy is required.
